title: mentha sylvestris subsp. mollissima
intro: Mentha sylvestris subsp. mollissima, also known as woolly mint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant, characterized by its soft and hairy leaves. This plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Mentha sylvestris subsp. mollissima include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, as well as its ability to soothe respiratory issues like coughs and colds. Mentha sylvestris subsp. mollissima can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
